vllm.title: [DCP] Support dcp kv_cache interleave size > 1
vllm.author: zhangsicheng5
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
1. cp_kv_cache_interleave_size support
In dcp scenario, kv_cache is split across dcp ranks, current implementation (#23734) split kv_cache with a token-level interleave style: token_idx i is stored on GPU whose dcp_rank == i % dcp_world_size.
For the convenience of pd disaggregate support, w

Purpose
Added support for multi-NIC configurations by parsing VLLM_HOST_IP from additional arguments. Built Ray IP environment variables to ensure correct network interface binding.
Test Plan
Run on device with multiple interfaces (such as DGX Spark) . The desired behavior is that the IP address of

vllm.title: [Feat] Drop-in Torch CUDA Profiler
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR adds a CUDA profiler via torch.cuda.profiler, which integrates natively with the existing start_profile infrastructure.
It is very easy to use. On NVIDIA GPUs:
export VLLM_TORCH_CUDA_PROFILE=1
nsys profile -o report.nsys-rep \
  --trace-fork-before-exec=true \
  --cuda-graph-trace=no

vllm.title: Remove setuptools upper bound constraint (<80)
vllm.author: ColeMurray
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #28336
Summary
Removes the <80.0.0 upper bound constraint on setuptools to resolve dependency conflicts with packages that require setuptools 80+.
Changes

Updated pyproject.toml: Changed setuptools>=77.0.3,<80.0.0 to setuptools>=77.0.3
Updated requirements/build.txt: Changed setuptools>=77.0.
